xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Summarise expression distribution of a reference data frame

Source: R/summarize_expression.R
summarize_expression.Rd

Inspects a reference data frame (HPA RNA, Tabula Sapiens, or similar) to help choose cutoff_type and cutoff_value before calling enrich_by_hpa or enrich_by_tabula. Returns per-group expression summaries and a preview of how many genes each cutoff combination would select.

summarize_expression(
  data,
  group_col,
  gene_col = "Gene.name",
  expr_col = "pTPM",
  cutoff_preview = list(list(type = "quantile", value = 0.9), list(type = "threshold",
    value = 1), list(type = "top_k", value = 200))
)

Arguments

data

Data frame in long format. Must contain group_col, gene_col, and expr_col.

group_col

Character. Column name for the grouping variable (e.g. "Tissue", "Brain.region", "cell_type").

gene_col

Character. Column name for gene identifiers. Default "Gene.name".

expr_col

Character. Column name for expression values. Default "pTPM".

cutoff_preview

Named list of cutoff specifications. Each element is a list with type (one of "quantile", "threshold", "top_k") and value. Defaults cover the three most common choices.

Value

A list with two elements:

distribution

Data frame with one row per group: group, n_genes, median_expr, n_above_p75, n_above_p90, n_above_p95.

cutoff_preview

Data frame with one row per group-cutoff combination: group, cutoff_type, cutoff_value, n_selected, pct_of_universe.

Examples

# Simulate a minimal HPA-style long data frame
set.seed(1)
ref <- data.frame(
  Gene.name = rep(paste0("G", 1:100), times = 3),
  Tissue    = rep(c("Liver", "Kidney", "Brain"), each = 100),
  pTPM      = c(rexp(100, 0.05), rexp(100, 0.02), rexp(100, 0.1))
)

sm <- summarize_expression(ref, group_col = "Tissue")
sm$distribution
#>    group n_genes median_expr n_above_p75 n_above_p90 n_above_p95
#> 1  Brain     100       6.837          25          10           5
#> 2 Kidney     100      38.129          25          10           5
#> 3  Liver     100      16.175          25          10           5
sm$cutoff_preview
#>    group cutoff_type cutoff_value n_selected pct_of_universe
#> 1  Brain    quantile          0.9         10              10
#> 2  Brain   threshold            1         91              91
#> 3  Brain       top_k          200        100             100
#> 4 Kidney    quantile          0.9         10              10
#> 5 Kidney   threshold            1        100             100
#> 6 Kidney       top_k          200        100             100
#> 7  Liver    quantile          0.9         10              10
#> 8  Liver   threshold            1         99              99
#> 9  Liver       top_k          200        100             100
